steps.title: Identify the core of the claim
steps.content: The student's claim hinges on “significant change in Hepworth's artistic development”—a developmental turning point in her work. The supporting quotation must establish evidence of this shift, not merely assert that the piece is important or well-executed.

steps.title: Match evidence of change
steps.content: Choice A directly supports a developmental shift by establishing that Hepworth's sculptures before Pierced Form belonged to an “earlier style, to which she never returned”—proving a clear before/after change in her trajectory. Choices B, C, and D respectively address her influence/originality, the work's interpretive complexity, and its representativeness, none of which show a developmental shift. That matches choice A — A is correct.
